channel.title: Blog 1 on Brennan Ryan Profile
channel.description: Recent content in Blog 1 on Brennan Ryan Profile

item.title: Blog 1
item.description:
An Implication of Ethical Complication

Aristotle’s Nicomachean Ethic states that moral behaviour is between 2 extremes:

  • Excess (using all available data, without ethical concern)
  • Deficiency (not using any data at all) Both of these extremes pose significant drawbacks, the former (excess) being unethical and risking any and all privacy of data. The latter (deficiency) being so cautious as to not gather data at all prevents any form of progress and innovation. This is where a concept known as the ethics equilibrium comes into play, a balance between the two extremes so as to foster growth while protecting the data privacy of everyone involved.

An Ethical Conundrum
